title: Suunnistus
description: Learn Finnish direction vocabulary and how to give and understand directions
instructions: Practice direction words, location terms, and imperative forms used when giving directions in Finnish.

questions.text: What does 'vasemmalle' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Vasemmalle' = to the left (direction of movement, allative). 'Vasemmalla' = on the left (location, adessive). Example: Käänny vasemmalle! (Turn to the left!)

questions.text: What does 'oikealla' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Oikealla' = on the right (location). 'Oikealle' = to the right (direction). The -lla ending shows location; -lle shows direction toward.

questions.text: What does 'eteenpäin' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Eteenpäin' = forward / straight ahead. 'Eteen' = to the front, 'päin' = toward. Used in 'kävele eteenpäin' (walk forward).

questions.text: What is a 'risteys' in Finnish?
questions.explanation: 'Risteys' = crossing / intersection. 'Ensimmäisestä risteyksestä oikealle' = turn right at the first intersection.

questions.text: What are 'liikennevalot'?
questions.explanation: 'Liikennevalot' = traffic lights. 'Liikenne' = traffic, 'valot' = lights (plural of valo).

questions.text: What is the difference between 'vasemmalla' and 'vasemmalle'?
options: No difference — they mean the same thing
Vasemmalla = on the left (location); vasemmalle = to the left (direction toward)
Vasemmalla = turn left; vasemmalle = go left
Vasemmalla is formal; vasemmalle is informal
questions.explanation: Key distinction! -lla/-llä = location (adessive): vasemmalla = on the left (where it is). -lle/-lle = direction toward (allative): vasemmalle = to the left (where you're going).

questions.text: What does 'suoraan' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Suoraan' = straight. Often used with 'eteenpäin': 'mene suoraan eteenpäin' (go straight ahead). 'Suoraan' can also mean 'directly' in other contexts.

questions.text: What does 'lähellä' mean?
questions.explanation: 'Lähellä' = close by / nearby. Opposite: 'kaukana' = far away. Example: 'Onko se lähellä?' (Is it nearby?)

questions.text: How do you say '[X distance] away' in Finnish?
questions.explanation: [distance] + päässä = [X] away. Example: 'kaksi kilometriä päässä' = two kilometers away. 'Päässä' comes from 'pää' (end/head).

questions.text: What does 'ohi' mean in directions?
questions.explanation: 'Ohi' = past / by. Example: 'Mene kaupan ohi' (Go past the store). 'Ohi' indicates passing something without entering.

questions.text: How do you form the imperative for directions? (from 'mennä' - to go)
options: Take mä form, remove n: menet → mene!
questions.explanation: Imperative for directions: take the sä form (you), remove the personal ending -t. mennä → sä menet → remove -t → Mene! (Go!)

questions.text: What is the imperative of 'kävellä' (to walk)?
questions.explanation: kävellä → sä kävelet → remove -t → Kävele! (Walk!). This is the direction-giving imperative.

questions.text: What is the imperative of 'kääntyä' (to turn)?
questions.explanation: kääntyä → sä käännyt → remove -t → Käänny! (Turn!). Note the vowel change: kääntyä → käännyt → käänny.

questions.text: How do you say 'Don't turn!' in Finnish (negative imperative)?
questions.explanation: Negative imperative: add 'älä' before the positive imperative form. Käänny! (Turn!) → Älä käänny! (Don't turn!)

questions.text: How do you say 'Walk!' in Finnish (as a direction instruction)?
questions.explanation: 'Kävele!' = Walk! (imperative). The informal singular imperative is used for giving directions to one person.

questions.text: How do you say 'Turn right at the first intersection'?
questions.explanation: 'Käänny ensimmäisestä risteyksestä oikealle.' Ensimmäisestä risteyksestä = from the first intersection (elative). Oikealle = to the right (allative).

questions.text: In the sample directions: 'Käänny pohjoiseen. Kävele eteenpäin.' What do these two sentences mean?
questions.explanation: 'Käänny' = turn (from kääntyä). 'Pohjoiseen' = to the north (illative: pohjoinen → pohjoiseen). 'Kävele eteenpäin' = walk forward. So: Turn north. Walk forward.

questions.text: 'Olet perillä!' — what does this phrase mean?
questions.explanation: 'Olet perillä!' = You have arrived! / You're there! 'Perillä' is an adverb meaning 'at the destination'. Used at the end of giving directions.

questions.text: How do you say 'straight ahead' in Finnish?
questions.explanation: 'Eteenpäin' or 'suoraan eteenpäin' = straight ahead. Both are commonly used in directions.

questions.text: Which imperative form is used for giving directions to one person?
questions.explanation: The singular imperative (kävele!, käänny!, mene!) is used for directions to one person. This is the most natural form for giving directions.

questions.text: What is the Finnish word for 'street'?
questions.explanation: 'Katu' = street (in a city). 'Tie' = road (more general, often outside cities). 'Reitti' = route. 'Polku' = path/trail.

questions.text: Form the imperative of 'mennä' (to go): sä menet → _____!
questions.explanation: mennä → sä menet → remove -t → Mene! (Go!). The imperative is just the sä form without the -t ending.

questions.text: How do you give this instruction: 'Walk past the store'?
questions.explanation: 'Kävele kaupan ohi.' = Walk past the store. 'Ohi' = past/by. 'Kaupan' is the genitive of 'kauppa' (store) — genitive is used before postpositions like 'ohi'.
